title: Loeseneriella rowlandii
taxonomy: Loeseneriella rowlandii is a species of plant in the family Celastraceae. It is a flowering plant native to New Guinea. The plant has been classified into various ranks within the botanical hierarchy, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species. Loeseneriella rowlandii belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Celastrales, family Celastraceae, and genus Loeseneriella. Further classification may vary depending on the source and the specific taxonomy used.

intro: Loeseneriella rowlandii, also known as Rowland's Loeseneriella, is a plant species in the family Celastraceae, native to the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, suggesting potential health benefits in treating various conditions. The bioactive constituents of Loeseneriella rowlandii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
